definition: Ageing and dying as the culminating suffering conditioned by rebirth in the dependent-arising sequence.
notes: This entry uses ageing and dying as the house default because it reads naturally in English while preserving the compound movement of the term. Old age and death remains the literal anchor, and aging-and-death remains available where the tighter doctrinal compound is preferred. The term should be heard as the culminating afflicted outcome of the chain, along with the standard expansion into sorrow and related forms of distress.

context_rules.context: In more literal or tightly formulaic doctrinal prose
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when the compound structure should remain more explicit.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where a familiar phrase helps
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate outside tightly patterned formula translation.

context_rules.context: When the passage emphasizes the culminating suffering that follows rebirth in the sequence
context_rules.notes: Keep the place of the link as the terminal compound explicit.

example_phrases.translation: with rebirth as condition, ageing and dying
example_phrases.notes: Closing link of the standard sequence.

example_phrases.translation: sorrow, lamentation, dissatisfaction, distress, and despair come to be
example_phrases.notes: Standard expansion following ageing and dying in the formula.
